title: Retrieve  publication entries from Google Scholar and sort chronologically.

markdown.headingText: Retrieve  publication entries from Google Scholar and sort chronologically.
markdown.markdown:
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} library(scholar) library(tidyverse) id <- 'v3VYtRkAAAAJ&hl' pubs <- scholar::get_publications(id) pubs <- pubs[rev(order(pubs$year)), ] urls<-scholar::get_article_scholar_url(str_remove(id,"&hl$"), pubs$pubid) pubs$urls <- urls pubs|>write_csv("pubs.csv") abst<-pubs|>filter(str_detect(journal,"Drug and Alcohol")) pubs<-pubs|>filter(!str_detect(journal,"Drug and Alcohol")) # Format entries as a markdown unordered list. pubs_lst <- data.frame( ref = paste0( '[', pubs$title, '](', pubs$urls, ').', '
', pubs$author, '.', '
', pubs$journal, ', ', pubs$year, '.' ) ) abst_lst <- data.frame( ref = paste0( '[', abst$title, '](', abst$urls, ').', '
', abst$author, '.', '
', abst$journal, ', ', abst$year, '.' ) ) pubs_lst$ref <- stringr::str_replace_all(pubs_lst$ref, 'A(lec)?\\s*Bodolay', '**\\0**') abst_lst$ref <- stringr::str_replace_all(abst_lst$ref, 'A(lec)?\\s*Bodolay', '**\\0**') ``` ### Journal ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[1\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[2]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[2\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[1]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[3\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[3]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[4\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[4]` ::: ::: ### Abstracts ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[1\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[3]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[2\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[1]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[3\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[4]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[4\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[5]` ::: :::
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} library(scholar) library(tidyverse) # Retrieve publication entries from Google Scholar and sort chronologically. id <- 'v3VYtRkAAAAJ&hl' pubs <- scholar::get_publications(id) pubs <- pubs[rev(order(pubs$year)), ] urls<-scholar::get_article_scholar_url(str_remove(id,"&hl$"), pubs$pubid) pubs$urls <- urls pubs|>write_csv("pubs.csv") abst<-pubs|>filter(str_detect(journal,"Drug and Alcohol")) pubs<-pubs|>filter(!str_detect(journal,"Drug and Alcohol")) # Format entries as a markdown unordered list. pubs_lst <- data.frame( ref = paste0( '[', pubs$title, '](', pubs$urls, ').', '
', pubs$author, '.', '
', pubs$journal, ', ', pubs$year, '.' ) ) abst_lst <- data.frame( ref = paste0( '[', abst$title, '](', abst$urls, ').', '
', abst$author, '.', '
', abst$journal, ', ', abst$year, '.' ) ) pubs_lst$ref <- stringr::str_replace_all(pubs_lst$ref, 'A(lec)?\\s*Bodolay', '**\\0**') abst_lst$ref <- stringr::str_replace_all(abst_lst$ref, 'A(lec)?\\s*Bodolay', '**\\0**') ``` ### Journal ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[1\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[2]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[2\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[1]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[3\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[3]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[4\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r pubs_lst$ref[4]` ::: ::: ### Abstracts ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[1\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[3]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[2\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[1]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[3\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[4]` ::: ::: ::: columns ::: {.column width="5%"} \[4\] ::: ::: {.column width="95%"} `r abst_lst$ref[5]` ::: :::
